title: Bupleurum rohlenae
intro: Bupleurum rohlenae, also known as Rohlena's hare's ear or Rohlena's knotted hare's ear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and liver disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Bupleurum rohlenae include flavonoids, saponins, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Bupleurum rohlenae is commonly used in the form of dried root or root extract, often combined with other herbs such as ginseng and licorice root, to make teas, capsules, or decoctions.
taxonomy: Bupleurum rohlenae is classified as a species within the genus Bupleurum, which is part of the family Apiaceae. The genus Bupleurum belongs to the order Apiales. The family Apiaceae is a part of the clade Magnoliidae, which is a group of flowering plants. This classification follows the APG IV plant classification system. Bupleurum rohlenae is a unique species that is native to the Czech Republic.
uses: Bupleurum rohlenae, also known as Rohlena's hare's ear, has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Eastern European cultures. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant has been used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. However, its medicinal uses are not extensively documented in modern medical literature, and more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications. Further studies are required to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Bupleurum rohlenae for medicinal purposes.

The plant is particularly known for its high content of saikosaponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties.
